channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Critical Nginx UI flaw CVE-2026-27944 exposes server backups
item.description: Nginx UI flaw CVE-2026-27944 lets attackers download and decrypt server backups without authentication, exposing sensitive data on public management interfaces. A critical vulnerability in Nginx UI, tracked as CVE-202...

item.title: Hackers abuse .arpa DNS and ipv6 to evade phishing defenses
item.description: Threat actors are abusing the special-use ".arpa" domain and IPv6 reverse DNS in phishing campaigns that more easily evade domain reputation checks and email security gateways. [...]

item.title: Massive GitHub malware operation spreads BoryptGrab stealer
item.description: Trend Micro found BoryptGrab stealer spreading through 100+ GitHub repositories, stealing browser data, crypto wallets, system information, and user files. Trend Micro uncovered a campaign distributing the BoryptGrab...

item.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 87
item.description: Security Affairs Malware newsletter includes a collection of the best articles and research on malware in the international landscape Malware Newsletter Malware Reverse Engineering is no longer a human problem! StegaB...

item.title: Week in review: Weaponized OAuth redirection logic delivers malware, Patch Tuesday forecast
item.description: Here’s an overview of some of last week’s most interesting news, articles, interviews and videos: BlacksmithAI: Open-source AI-powered penetration testing framework BlacksmithAI is an open-source penetration testing f...

item.title: OpenAI Codex Security Scanned 1.2 Million Commits and Found 10,561 High-Severity Issues
item.description: OpenAI on Friday began rolling out Codex Security, an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered security agent that's designed to find, validate, and propose fixes for vulnerabilities. The feature is available in a researc...

item.title: Over 100 GitHub Repositories Distributing BoryptGrab Stealer
item.description: The malware targets browser and cryptocurrency wallet data, along with system information and user files. The post Over 100 GitHub Repositories Distributing BoryptGrab Stealer appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Anthropic Finds 22 Firefox Vulnerabilities Using Claude Opus 4.6 AI Model
item.description: Anthropic on Friday said it discovered 22 new security vulnerabilities in the Firefox web browser as part of a security partnership with Mozilla. Of these, 14 have been classified as high, seven have been classified a...

item.title: Iran-linked MuddyWater deploys Dindoor malware against U.S. organizations
item.description: Iran-linked APT MuddyWater targeted U.S. organizations, deploying the new Dindoor backdoor across sectors including banks, airports, and nonprofits. Broadcom’s Symantec Threat Hunter Team uncovered a campaign by the I...

item.title: Cognizant TriZetto breach exposes health data of 3.4 million patients
item.description: TriZetto Provider Solutions, a healthcare IT company that develops software and services used by health insurers and healthcare providers, has suffered a data breach that exposed the sensitive information of over 3.4...

item.title: Cylake Offers AI-Native Security Without Relying on Cloud Services
item.description: Cylake's platform will analyze security data locally and identify potential attacks for organizations concerned about data sovereignty.

item.title: Cisco flags ongoing exploitation of two recently patched Catalyst SD-WAN flaws
item.description: Cisco warns that two recently patched Catalyst SD-WAN flaws, CVE-2026-20128 and CVE-2026-20122, are already being actively exploited in the wild. Cisco warned customers that threat actors are actively exploiting two r...

item.title: Transparent Tribe Uses AI to Mass-Produce Malware Implants in Campaign Targeting India
item.description: The Pakistan-aligned threat actor known as Transparent Tribe has become the latest hacking group to embrace artificial intelligence (AI)-powered coding tools to strike targets with various implants. The activity is de...

item.title: EU Auto Rules Shift Gears on Cybersecurity Standards
item.description: The European Union is taking new precautions as climate change and cybersecurity threats rise across the automotive industry.

item.title: EC-Council Expands AI Certification Portfolio to Strengthen U.S. AI Workforce Readiness and Security
item.description: EC-Council, creator of the world-renowned Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH) credential and a global leader in applied cybersecurity education, today launched its Enterprise AI Credential Suite, with four new role-based A...

item.title: Fake Claude Code install guides push infostealers in InstallFix attacks
item.description: Threat actors are employing a new variation of the ClickFix social engineering technique called InstallFix to convince users into running malicious commands under the pretext of installing legitimate command line inte...

item.title: Multi-Stage VOID#GEIST Malware Delivering XWorm, AsyncRAT, and Xeno RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a multi-stage malware campaign that uses batch scripts as a pathway to deliver various encrypted remote access trojan (RATs) payloads that correspond to XWorm, Async...

item.title: From the endpoint to the prompt: a unified data security vision in Cloudflare One
item.description: Cloudflare One unifies data security from endpoint to prompt: RDP clipboard controls, operation-mapped logs, on-device DLP, and Microsoft 365 Copilot scanning via API CASB.

item.title: Iran-linked APT targets US critical sectors with new backdoors
item.description: An Iran-linked hacking group has been active inside the networks of several US organizations since early February, raising concerns that the activity could precede broader cyber operations connected to escalating geop...

item.title: Cyberattack on Mexico's Gov't Agencies Highlight AI Threat
item.description: Using Anthropic's Claude, OpenAI's ChatGPT, and a detailed playbook prompt, a handful of cyberattackers reportedly gained access to government agencies and its citizens' data.

item.title: Microsoft working on Teams feature to keep unauthorized bots at bay
item.description: Microsoft plans to add a new Teams feature that lets meeting admins identify and control third-party bots before they join. According to the Microsoft 365 Roadmap, the feature is scheduled to begin rolling out in May...

item.title: CISA Adds iOS Flaws From Coruna Exploit Kit to KEV List
item.description: The nation-state-grade iOS exploit kit targets 23 vulnerabilities affecting iOS 13 to 17.2.1. The post CISA Adds iOS Flaws From Coruna Exploit Kit to KEV List appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Microsoft warns of ClickFix campaign exploiting Windows Terminal to deliver Lumma Stealer
item.description: Microsoft warns of ClickFix campaign using Windows Terminal to deliver Lumma Stealer via social engineering attacks. Microsoft revealed a new ClickFix campaign where attackers exploit Windows Terminal to run a complex...

item.title: An Investigation Into Years of Undetected Operations Targeting High-Value Sectors
item.description: In-depth analysis of threat activity we call CL-UNK-1068. We discuss their toolset, including tunneling, reconnaissance and credential theft. The post An Investigation Into Years of Undetected Operations Targeting Hig...

item.title: The MSP Guide to Using AI-Powered Risk Management to Scale Cybersecurity
item.description: Scaling cybersecurity services as an MSP or MSSP requires technical expertise and a business model that delivers measurable value at scale. Risk-based cybersecurity is the foundation of that model. When done right, it...

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5.4 doubles down on safety as competition heats up
item.description: In the midst of recent developments and controversies surrounding a contract with the U.S. Department of Defense, OpenAI released the GPT-5.4 model. The release comes at a time when users are reportedly leaving ChatGP...

item.title: FBI investigates breach of surveillance and wiretap systems
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) confirmed on Thursday that it's investigating a breach that affected systems used to manage surveillance and wiretap warrants. [...]

item.title: March 2026 Patch Tuesday forecast: Is AI security an oxymoron?
item.description: Developers and analysts are using more AI tools to produce code and to test both the performance and security of the finished products. They are also embedding AI functionality in their products directly. But just how...

item.title: Backup strategies are working, and ransomware gangs are responding with data theft
item.description: Business email compromise (BEC) and funds transfer fraud combined for 58% of all cyber insurance claims filed in 2025, according to data from Coalition covering more than 100,000 policyholders across the United States...

item.title: Microsoft Reveals ClickFix Campaign Using Windows Terminal to Deploy Lumma Stealer
item.description: Microsoft on Thursday disclosed details of a new widespread ClickFix social engineering campaign that has leveraged the Windows Terminal app as a way to activate a sophisticated attack chain and deploy the Lumma Steal...

item.title: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Hikvision and Rockwell Automation products to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing e...

item.title: Wikipedia hit by self-propagating JavaScript worm that vandalized pages
item.description: The Wikimedia Foundation suffered a security incident today after a self-propagating JavaScript worm began vandalizing pages and modifying user scripts across multiple wikis. [...]

item.title: Cisco Drops 48 New Firewall Vulnerabilities, 2 Critical
item.description: Edge bugs are so fetch, and Cisco just patched 50 new ones, including some heavy hitters with 10 out of 10 scores on the CVSS scale.

item.title: Fig Security Emerges From Stealth to Fix Broken Security Operations
item.description: Fig Security's platform traces security data flows end-to-end across SIEMs, pipelines, and response systems to alert teams before infrastructure changes break critical defenses.

item.title: Cisco Confirms Active Exploitation of Two Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Vulnerabilities
item.description: Cisco has disclosed that two more vulnerabilities affecting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly SD-WAN vManage) have come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below - CVE-202...

item.title: Preparing for the Quantum Era: Post-Quantum Cryptography Webinar for Security Leaders
item.description: Most organizations assume encrypted data is safe. But many attackers are already preparing for a future where today’s encryption can be broken. Instead of trying to decrypt information now, they are collecting encrypt...

item.title: Ending the "silent drop": how Dynamic Path MTU Discovery makes the Cloudflare One Client more resilient
item.description: The Cloudflare One Client now features the ability to actively probe and adjust packet sizes. This update eliminates the problems caused by tunnel layering and MTU differences, providing more stability and resiliency.

item.title: LatAm Now Faces 2x More Cyberattacks Than US
item.description: Much of Central and South America struggles with cybersecurity maturity, and hackers are taking advantage.

item.title: ContextCrush Flaw Exposes AI Development Tools to Attacks
item.description: Critical flaw "ContextCrush" in Context7 MCP Server could allow malicious instructions into AI tools

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: DDR5 Bot Scalping, Samsung TV Tracking, Reddit Privacy Fine & More
item.description: Some weeks in cybersecurity feel routine. This one doesn’t. Several new developments surfaced over the past few days, showing how quickly the threat landscape keeps shifting. Researchers uncovered fresh activity, secu...

item.title: Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New SPLITDROP and GHOSTFORM Malware
item.description: A suspected Iran-nexus threat actor has been attributed to a campaign targeting government officials in Iraq by impersonating the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs to deliver a set of never-before-seen malware. Zs...

item.title: Where Multi-Factor Authentication Stops and Credential Abuse Starts
item.description: Organizations typically roll out multi-factor authentication (MFA) and assume stolen passwords are no longer enough to access systems. In Windows environments, that assumption is often wrong. Attackers still compromis...

item.title: APT28-Linked Campaign Deploys BadPaw Loader and MeowMeow Backdoor in Ukraine
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Russian cyber campaign that has targeted Ukrainian entities with two previously undocumented malware families named BadPaw and MeowMeow. "The attack chain init...

item.title: Europol-Led Operation Takes Down Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Linked to 64,000 Attacks
item.description: Tycoon 2FA, one of the prominent phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) toolkits that allowed cybercriminals to stage adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) credential harvesting attacks at scale, was dismantled by a coalition of law...

item.title: FBI and Europol Seize LeakBase Forum Used to Trade Stolen Credentials
item.description: A joint law enforcement operation has dismantled LeakBase, one of the world's largest online forums for cybercriminals to buy and sell stolen data and cybercrime tools. The LeakBase forum, per the U.S. Department of J...

item.title: A QUICker SASE client: re-building Proxy Mode
item.description: By transitioning the Cloudflare One Client to use QUIC streams for Proxy Mode, we eliminated the overhead of user-space TCP stacks, resulting in a 2x increase in throughput and significant latency reduction for end us...

item.title: VMware Aria Operations Bug Exploited, Cloud Resources at Risk
item.description: Exploitation of the command injection flaw in VMware Aria Operations could grant an attacker broad acess to victims' cloud environments.

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The hacktivist threat in...

item.title: Multi-Stage "BadPaw" Malware Campaign Targets Ukraine
item.description: Malware campaign uses Ukrainian email service for credibility, deploying "BadPaw" to execute attacks

item.title: Mind the gap: new tools for continuous enforcement from boot to login
item.description: Cloudflare’s mandatory authentication and independent MFA protect organizations by ensuring continuous enforcement, from the moment a machine boots until sensitive resources are accessed.

item.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Uses 23 Exploits Across Five Chains Targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
item.description: Google said it identified a "new and powerful" exploit kit dubbed Coruna (aka CryptoWaters) targeting Apple iPhone models running iOS versions between 13.0 and 17.2.1. The exploit kit featured five full iOS exploit ch...

item.title: New RFP Template for AI Usage Control and AI Governance
item.description: As AI becomes the central engine for enterprise productivity, security leaders are finally getting the green light — and the budget — to secure it. But there’s a quiet crisis unfolding in the boardroom: many organizat...

item.title: China's Silver Dragon Razes Governments in EU, SE Asia
item.description: The emerging actor, part of the APT41 nexus, gains initial access via phishing, and uses legitimate network services to obscure cyber espionage activities.

item.title: Fake Laravel Packages on Packagist Deploy RAT on Windows, macOS, and Linux
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged malicious Packagist PHP packages masquerading as Laravel utilities that act as a conduit for a cross-platform remote access trojan (RAT) that's functional on Windows, macOS, and...

item.title: APT41-Linked Silver Dragon Targets Governments Using Cobalt Strike and Google Drive C2
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an advanced persistent threat (APT) group dubbed Silver Dragon that has been linked to cyber attacks targeting entities in Europe and Southeast Asia since at least m...

item.title: Defeating the deepfake: stopping laptop farms and insider threats
item.description: Cloudflare One is partnering with Nametag to combat laptop farms and AI-enhanced identity fraud by requiring identity verification during employee onboarding and via continuous authentication.

item.title: Moving from license plates to badges: the Gateway Authorization Proxy
item.description: Cloudflare’s Gateway Authorization Proxy adds support for identity-aware policies for clientless devices, securing virtual desktops, and guest networks without a device client.

item.title: Stop reacting to breaches and start preventing them with User Risk Scoring
item.description: Cloudflare One now incorporates dynamic User Risk Scores into Access policies to enable automated, adaptive security responses. This update allows teams to move beyond binary "allow/deny" rules by evaluating continuou...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited VMware Aria Operations Flaw CVE-2026-22719 to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting Broadcom VMware Aria Operations to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citi...

item.title: Qualcomm Zero-Day Exploited in Targeted Android Attacks
item.description: The exploitation activity against CVE-2026-21385, a high-severity memory corruption flaw, could be tied to commercial spyware or nation-state threat groups.

item.title: 2025 FINMA ISAE 3000 Type II attestation report available with 183 services in scope
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pleased to announce the issuance of the Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority (FINMA) Type II attestation report with 183 services in scope. The Swiss Financial Market Supervisory A...

item.title: Speakeasies to Shadow AI: Banning AI Browsers Will Fail
item.description: Lessons from history highlight why AI-enabled browsers require controlled enablement.

item.title: Fake Tech Support Spam Deploys Customized Havoc C2 Across Organizations
item.description: Threat hunters have called attention to a new campaign as part of which bad actors masqueraded as fake IT support to deliver the Havoc command-and-control (C2) framework as a precursor to data exfiltration or ransomwa...

item.title: AI and Deepfakes Supercharge Sophisticated Cyber-Attacks, Says Cloudflare
item.description: Cloudflare Threat Report warns that AI tools enable attackers who lacked required skills to generate effective attacks rapidly and at scale

item.title: Bruteforce Scans for CrushFTP , (Tue, Mar 3rd)
item.description: CrushFTP is a Java-based open source file transfer system. It is offered for multiple operating systems. If you run a CrushFTP instance, you may remember that the software has had some serious vulnerabilities: CVE-202...

item.title: Building a High-Impact Tier 1: The 3 Steps CISOs Must Follow
item.description: Every CISO knows the uncomfortable truth about their Security Operations Center: the people most responsible for catching threats in real time are the people with the least experience. Tier 1 analysts sit at the front...

item.title: Open-Source CyberStrikeAI Deployed in AI-Driven FortiGate Attacks Across 55 Countries
item.description: The threat actor behind the recently disclosed artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate appliances leveraged an open-source, AI-native security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI...

item.title: Introducing the 2026 Cloudflare Threat Report
item.description: There has been a fundamental shift toward industrialized cyber threats, highlighted by a record 31.4 Tbps DDoS attack and sophisticated session token theft. Our new report examines how nation-states and criminal actor...

item.title: Evolving Cloudflare’s Threat Intelligence Platform: actionable, scalable, and ETL-less
item.description: Stop managing ETL pipelines and start threat hunting. Introducing new visualization, automation, and enrichment tools in the Cloudflare Threat Intelligence Platform to turn massive telemetry into instant security post...

item.title: Half of US CISOs Work the Equivalent of a Six-Day Week
item.description: Seemplicity finds US security leaders work 11 or more extra hours per week

item.title: AI Agent Overload: How to Solve the Workload Identity Crisis
item.description: Workloads keep getting more complicated and organizations are struggling to keep up. So what's the play?

item.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
item.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. By providing structured access to applications, APIs, and data, MCP enab...

item.title: Starkiller Phishing Suite Uses AitM Reverse Proxy to Bypass Multi-Factor Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new phishing suite called Starkiller that proxies legitimate login pages to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) protections. It's advertised as a cybercrime p...

item.title: Fooling AI Agents: Web-Based Indirect Prompt Injection Observed in the Wild
item.description: Uncover real-world indirect prompt injection attacks and learn how adversaries weaponize hidden web content to exploit LLMs for high-impact fraud. The post Fooling AI Agents: Web-Based Indirect Prompt Injection Observ...

item.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
item.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The activity, the company s...

item.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-21385 (CVSS s...

item.title: SloppyLemming Targets Pakistan and Bangladesh Governments Using Dual Malware Chains
item.description: The threat activity cluster known as SloppyLemming has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting government entities and critical infrastructure operators in Pakistan and Bangladesh. The activity, per Arctic...

item.title: How Cloudy translates complex security into human action
item.description: Cloudy is our LLM-powered explanation layer built directly into Cloudflare One. Its explanations, now part of Phishnet and API CASB, can improve user decisions and SOC efficiency.

item.title: From reactive to proactive: closing the phishing gap with LLMs
item.description: Email security is a constant arms race. Like WWII engineers reinforcing only the planes that returned, survivorship bias hides real gaps. But LLMs can help us find the invisible weaknesses.

item.title: See risk, fix risk: introducing Remediation in Cloudflare CASB
item.description: Cloudflare CASB Remediation lets security teams go beyond visibility to fix risky file sharing in Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace directly from Cloudflare One, all in just a few clicks.

item.title: Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation of Cyber Risk Related to Iran
item.description: Unit 42 details recent Iranian cyberattack activity, sharing direct observations of phishing, hacktivist activity and cybercrime. We include recommendations for defenders. The post Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation...

item.title: The Tug-of-War Over Firewall Backlogs in the AI-Driven Development Era
item.description: Speed and security are historically clashing priorities, but with AI and automation, it's increasingly important that application developers and security teams get on the same page.

item.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system. The vulnerabili...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: SD-WAN 0-Day, Critical CVEs, Telegram Probe, Smart TV Proxy SDK and More
item.description: This week is not about one big event. It shows where things are moving. Network systems, cloud setups, AI tools, and common apps are all being pushed in different ways. Small gaps in access control, exposed keys, and...

item.title: Quantum-Resistant Data Diode Secures Sensitive Data on Edge Devices, Critical Systems
item.description: Forward Edge-AI's new Isidore Quantum is a compact, low-power hardware device designed to defend sensitive operational technology endpoints against future quantum attacks.

item.title: Taming Agentic Browsers: Vulnerability in Chrome Allowed Extensions to Hijack New Gemini Panel
item.description: A high-severity CVE-2026-0628 in Chrome's Gemini allowed local file access and privacy invasion. Google quickly patched the flaw. The post Taming Agentic Browsers: Vulnerability in Chrome Allowed Extensions to Hijack...

item.title: APT28 Tied to CVE-2026-21513 MSHTML 0-Day Exploited Before Feb 2026 Patch Tuesday
item.description: A recently disclosed security flaw patched by Microsoft may have been exploited by the Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor known as APT28, according to new findings from Akamai. The vulnerability in question is...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new iteration of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, where the North Korean threat actors have published a set of 26 malicious packages to the npm registry. The packag...

item.title: Modernizing with agile SASE: a Cloudflare One blog takeover
item.description: In 2026, agile SASE is the engine for modernization. Discover how Cloudflare One secures humans, devices, and AI agents on a single, programmable connectivity cloud.

item.title: Beyond the blank slate: how Cloudflare accelerates your Zero Trust journey
item.description: Project Helix simplifies and accelerates the onboarding process for Cloudflare One. By using automation and Terraform templates, this tool allows customers to quickly deploy a comprehensive, best-practice configuratio...

item.title: The truly programmable SASE platform
item.description: As the only SASE platform with a native developer stack, we’re giving you the tools to build custom, real-time security logic and integrations directly at the edge.

item.title: CrowdStrike Achieves NCSC CIR Assurance for Incident Response
ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
item.description: OpenClaw has fixed a high-severity security issue that, if successfully exploited, could have allowed a malicious website to connect to a locally running artificial intelligence (AI) agent and take over control. "Our...

item.title: Thousands of Public Google Cloud API Keys Exposed with Gemini Access After API Enablement
item.description: New research has found that Google Cloud API keys, typically designated as project identifiers for billing purposes, could be abused to authenticate to sensitive Gemini endpoints and access private data. The findings...

item.title: Pentagon Designates Anthropic Supply Chain Risk Over AI Military Dispute
item.description: Anthropic on Friday hit back after U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth directed the Pentagon to designate the artificial intelligence (AI) upstart as a "supply chain risk." "This action follows months of negotiatio...

item.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
item.description: HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.

item.title: Cities Hosting Major Events Need More Focus on Wireless, Drone Defense
item.description: Major events like the FIFA World Cup need to look beyond traditional physical and cyber security to active and passive wireless threats, say experts.

item.title: Flaw-Finding AI Assistants Face Criticism for Speed, Accuracy
item.description: Using AI to find security vulnerabilities holds significant promise, but the initial products fall short of the needs of enterprises and software developers, say experts.

item.title: DoJ Seizes $61 Million in Tether Linked to Pig Butchering Crypto Scams
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) this week announced the seizure of $61 million worth of Tether that were allegedly associated with bogus cryptocurrency schemes known as pig butchering. The confiscated funds were...

item.title: 900+ Sangoma FreePBX Instances Compromised in Ongoing Web Shell Attacks
item.description: The Shadowserver Foundation has revealed that over 900 Sangoma FreePBX instances still remain infected with web shells as part of attacks that exploited a command injection vulnerability starting in December 2025. Of...

item.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
item.description: It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.

item.title: Malicious Go Crypto Module Steals Passwords, Deploys Rekoobe Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a malicious Go module that's designed to harvest passwords, create persistent access via SSH, and deliver a Linux backdoor named Rekoobe. The Go module, github[.]com...

item.title: ScarCruft Uses Zoho WorkDrive and USB Malware to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as ScarCruft has been attributed to a fresh set of tools, including a backdoor that uses Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control (C2) communications to fetch more payloads and an imp...

item.title: Meta Files Lawsuits Against Brazil, China, Vietnam Advertisers Over Celeb-Bait Scams
item.description: Meta on Thursday said it's taking legal action to tackle scams on its platforms by filing lawsuits against what it calls deceptive advertisers based in Brazil, China, and Vietnam. As part of the effort, the advertiser...

item.title: Toxic combinations: when small signals add up to a security incident
item.description: Minor misconfigurations or request anomalies often seem harmless in isolation. But when these small signals converge, they can trigger a security incident known as a toxic combination. Here’s how to spot the signs.

item.title: AWS successfully completed its first surveillance audit for ISO 42001:2023 with no findings
item.description: In November 2024, Amazon Web Services (AWS) was the first major cloud service provider to announce the ISO/IEC 42001 accredited certification for AI services, covering: Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Q Business, Amazon Textra...

item.title: Inside AWS Security Agent: A multi-agent architecture for automated penetration testing
item.description: AI agents have traditionally faced three core limitations: they can’t retain learned information or operate autonomously beyond short periods, and they require constant supervision. AWS addresses these limitations wit...

item.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
item.description: When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
item.description: The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.

item.title: Aeternum C2 Botnet Stores Encrypted Commands on Polygon Blockchain to Evade Takedown
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new botnet loader called Aeternum C2 that uses a blockchain-based command-and-control (C2) infrastructure to make it resilient to takedown efforts. "Instead of rel...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Kali Linux + Claude, Chrome Crash Traps, WinRAR Flaws, LockBit & 15+ Stories
item.description: Nothing here looks dramatic at first glance. That’s the point. Many of this week’s threats begin with something ordinary, like an ad, a meeting invite, or a software update. Behind the scenes, the tactics are sharper....

item.title: Expert Recommends: Prepare for PQC Right Now
item.description: Introduction: Steal It Today, Break It in a Decade Digital evolution is unstoppable, and though the pace may vary, things tend to fall into place sooner rather than later. That, of course, applies to adversaries as we...

item.title: Flaws in Claude Code Put Developers' Machines at Risk
item.description: The vulnerabilities highlight a big drawback to integrating AI into software development workflows and the potential impact on supply chains.

item.title: RAMP Forum Seizure Fractures Ransomware Ecosystem
item.description: Researchers suggest defenders monitor how these malicious groups re-form and leverage the useful threat intel to guide their next moves.

item.title: PCI Council Says Threats to Payments Systems Are Speeding Up
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council experienced a record year in many regards, but its first annual report shows it needs to work even faster to stay ahead of attackers.

item.title: Malicious Next.js Repos Target Developers Via Fake Job Interviews
item.description: Linked to North Korean fake job-recruitment campaigns, the poisoned repositories are aimed at establishing persistent access to infected machines.

item.title: 'Richter Scale' Model Measures Magnitude of OT Cyber Incidents
item.description: ICS/OT experts have devised a scoring system for rating the severity and effects of cybersecurity events in operational technology environments.

item.title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
item.description: Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.

item.title: Lazarus Group Picks a New Poison: Medusa Ransomware
item.description: The North Korean threat group also leveraged Comebacker backdoor, Blindingcan RAT, and info stealer Infohook in its recent attacks.

item.title: Bring the Fight to the Edge: Turning Time Into an Advantage in OT Security
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals most OT attacks begin in IT. Learn how edge-driven defense stops threats early and turns dwell time into advantage. The post Bring the Fight to the Edge: Turning Time Into an Advantage in OT S...
